Honest Advice From The Leaky Finders  ·  Lanzarote's Premium Leak Detection and Repair Company

When water appears where it definitely shouldn't, the panic is instant. The advice you actually need? Probably not what you'd expect from us.

You've just discovered water where water absolutely should not be. Perhaps it's a damp patch colonising your living room ceiling. Perhaps your water meter is spinning cheerfully despite nothing being switched on. Perhaps you've walked into the bathroom and the floor has developed an unexpected enthusiasm for being a swimming pool. Whatever it is, the feeling is immediate and universal. The stomach drops. The mind races. And the next thing you do matters more than you think.

The Call You Should Actually Make First

Before you call a leak detection company (including us) call your insurance company.

We have been saying this for years. Genuinely. Even when it costs us work, which it occasionally does, and which we are entirely at peace with because it happens to be the correct advice. If you have home insurance, there is a good chance your leak is covered, or at least partially covered. Your insurer needs to be involved regardless, and if you engage a contractor before making a claim, you risk complicating that claim significantly.

Call them first. Always. We've turned away bookings to make this point. We'll probably do it again.

A Word About Facebook This is going to sting slightly for some people.

Because here is what actually happens within thirty seconds: someone in the household announces they are going to post in the Facebook group. Please put the phone down.

Facebook groups are one of the great innovations of modern life. You can find a good dentist, sell a sofa, argue about planning permission, and discover that someone three streets over has been feeding your cat. Wonderful. But using a Facebook group to find a leak detection specialist is a bit like asking the pub for a recommendation on open heart surgery. The pub, at least, is honest about not being a doctor.

Here is a thing that happens in these comments sections, and we say this with no particular company in mind whatsoever: a meaningful proportion of those enthusiastic five-star recommendations are posted by people who have never experienced a leak in their lives. They are friends, family, people who owe a favour, and occasionally the business owner using a profile photo from 2014. The review has been engineered before a single pipe has been touched.

We are not saying everyone does it. We are saying you have no way of knowing who does and who doesn't. And given that a badly handled job can leave you with a demolished bathroom and a leak that is still leaking, the distinction matters.

"Any reputable company in this area will always point you towards your insurance company first. If a company's opening pitch is based on price, they probably have little else to offer."

Back to That Insurance Call. Did You Know? This is the part where things get interesting.

So you've stepped away from Facebook. Good. We have been telling people to call their insurance company first for years, including several years when it occasionally cost us a booking and we did it anyway. The advice has not changed. The punchline, however, has got considerably better.

After several months of applications, compliance checks, qualification vetting, and a quantity of Spanish paperwork we will not attempt to describe without emotional support nearby, Canary Detect has been officially approved as a registered leak detection provider for one of Spain's largest home assistance networks.

We are now live on a network covering policyholders from 21 major insurance companies operating across Lanzarote.

We didn't pitch for this. We didn't fill in an online form and cross our fingers. They came to us, based on our track record, our qualifications, and the working relationship we'd built with them informally over the years. When we say we are trusted by insurance companies, we mean it in a legally documented, compliance-checked, professionally vetted sense. Not the "they've never had cause to complain" sense.

So. When we say call your insurance first, there is now a very good chance that call ends with your insurer dispatching The Leaky Finders to your door anyway. Our advice hasn't changed. It was always the right call. It just has an increasingly happy ending.

If Your Insurer Says "Find Your Own Contractor" Here is the list. Print it out if you like.

Sometimes your insurer will instruct you to source your own approved contractor and submit the invoice. This is fine. What is not fine is picking up the phone to the first company that pops up in a Facebook comment without asking a single question. Before anyone opens a van door, ask the following:

The Five Questions Worth Asking Before You Book Anyone. Including Us.

  • Do they have qualified plumbers in-house? Always ask. Some companies operating in Lanzarote might be capable of finding your leak, but the closest they have to a plumbing qualification is a 50m breaststroke certificate from when they were 13. Get the answer in writing.
  • Are they specifically qualified in leak detection? Proper detection uses multiple technologies: acoustic listening, thermal imaging, tracer gas, pipe location and more. If their entire methodology is a one-sentence price promise, that should tell you something.
  • Can they manage the complete job: detection, excavation, repair, and full reinstatement? Finding it is the beginning, not the end.
  • Are they approved by any insurance networks or water authorities? Third-party vetting is not nothing.
  • Will they put everything in writing before they start? Scope, method, and price. All of it.
⚠  If you ask whether they have a qualified in-house team and they change the subject, that is your answer. You're welcome to ask us the same question. We enjoy it.

Finding the leak is only a small part of the job. What happens after is where it counts, and it is what separates the professionals from the people who leave you with two holes in the floor and a number for a tiler they vaguely know.

Some jobs are tidy. In and out, minimal disruption, done before lunch. And then some are a pig. Pipes routed through solid concrete in places nobody anticipated. Previous botched repairs that need undoing before you can even start. Access that requires significant portions of a bathroom to come up before you see anything useful. These jobs happen. In this business, they happen more regularly than the marketing team would prefer.

When it turns out to be one of those, you need a company that has seen it before and is not going to hand you a map and a phone number for a tiler. That is what in-house means. That is why it matters.

Yes, We're the Premium Option. We're Fine With That. Since you're going to ask about price anyway.

Canary Detect is not two blokes with expensive gadgets and a can-do attitude. We are a team of ten, qualified across every discipline this work involves: detection, plumbing, excavation, tiling, and full reinstatement. We have been operating on this island long enough to have found leaks for some of our customers' children. We do not say this to be alarming. We say it because track record matters, and ours is thirty years long and fully documented.

We pioneered the multi-technology, pipe-location-before-leak-location methodology in Lanzarote. We coined "no find, no fee" — a policy funded entirely from confidence, because we always find it. This has never been a problem. Which is either very impressive, or a sign that we are significantly better at this than we are at writing modest sentences about ourselves. Others have since adopted versions of our approach, which we note with the quiet satisfaction of people who built something worth copying. You can buy the same equipment. You cannot buy the expertise that tells you which piece to deploy and when.

"You can buy the same kit. You cannot buy the experience that tells you which bit to use and when. Several people have had a go."

We have 115 five-star Google reviews. From customers with actual leaks. Not one of them is from a cousin, a business partner, or a bloke who owed us a favour. You are welcome to read every single one, including the ones where the customer describes exactly what we found, where we found it, and how little of their floor we had to disturb in the process. That, incidentally, is why when a national network covering 21 insurance companies wanted a trusted local partner in Lanzarote, they came to us.

Trusted & Approved

National insurance network: approved registered provider for a network covering policyholders from 21 major insurance companies across Lanzarote. They vetted us. We passed.

Club Lanzarote: if your water supply is managed by Club Lanzarote, a rebate on your water bill is guaranteed on production of our technical report and invoice. Not "possible." Not "discretionary." Guaranteed.

Google reviews: 115 five-star reviews, all from customers with actual leaks. Every one of them verifiable.

One More Thing. About Catching It Earlier. You still need to fix it. We know.

Everything above assumes you have already found the problem. And when that happens, we are very good at what comes next. But we have also spent a long time thinking about the bit that comes before the phone call.

The leak that has been running silently inside a wall for three weeks. The pipe losing pressure slowly enough that nobody noticed until the bill arrived. The toilet passing water overnight, every night, for months. These are not dramatic emergencies. They are quiet ones. And they are often the most expensive kind, because by the time anyone knows about them, the damage has already done its work.

That thinking is behind LeakGuard, a proactive water monitoring system we have developed and are rolling out across Lanzarote. It watches your property's water consumption around the clock and flags anomalies early. There is a significant difference between finding out about a leak in week one, when the repair is contained and the bill is manageable, and finding out in month four, when the wall is wet, the floor is lifting, and Canal Gestión wants a conversation.

Property owners on the network can log in and see their consumption day by day and hour by hour: garden irrigation, pool usage, overnight toilet passing. But this is not simply a dashboard you are left to check yourself. We monitor the network too. Alarms are set on both sides, so when something anomalous happens, it does not sit quietly waiting for someone to notice it. It gets flagged. The kind of problem that used to run for weeks undetected gets caught in hours.

More at leakguardlanzarote.com.

And yes, you will find cheaper. We have seen surveys advertised for 199 euros. But the survey is rarely where the money is made. There is a model in this industry: a headline price to get through the door, and then a repair estimate that arrives once you've already committed. It works very well for the company. Slightly less so for the customer. We price the whole job honestly. The survey is not designed to get us through the door. The repair is not where we quietly recover the margin. From the first visit to the last tile, what we quote is what you pay. If you have insurance, your insurer may well cover a significant portion of the cost. If you don't, you are paying for a ten-person qualified team, full reinstatement, and a final bill that matches what we told you at the start. The survey fee is part of an honest price. That is why it is what it is.

So. Who Ya Gonna Call?

Your insurance company. First, always, without exception. Whether they send us or you choose us yourself, you call Canary Detect. The Leaky Finders. We'll be there before the towels dry out.
